issue_title: [OID4VCI] Credential Offer must be created by Issuer not Holder
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

The Holder should not be able to create credential offers - this is a task for the issuer.

In fact, there is no such thing as "Hey issuer, do you have a credential offer for me?"

Instead, there should be a service account that allows to create credential offers, which can then be passed on to the Holder's wallet endpoint

CrossRef: https://github.com/tdiesler/nessus-identity/issues/337

### Version

latest

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

See above

### Actual behavior

See above

### How to Reproduce?

OID4VCSdJwtIssuingEndpointTest.testCredentialIssuance

### Anything else?

You can assign this to me if you like

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

The test DockerClientTest is failing recently. The error is the following:

```
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5271268Z DockerClientTest ++ STDOUT: Using default tag: latest
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5272006Z DockerClientTest ++ The push refers to repository [localhost:5000/empty]
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5272635Z DockerClientTest ++ 
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5273017Z DockerClientTest ++ ---
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5274740Z DockerClientTest ++ STDERR: failed to authorize: failed to fetch oauth token: Post "https://172.17.0.1:8543/auth/realms/docker-test-realm/protocol/docker-v2/auth": tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: cannot validate certificate for 172.17.0.1 because it doesn't contain any IP SANs
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5276480Z DockerClientTest ++ 
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5277240Z DockerClientTest ++ 11:16:40,533 INFO  [org.keycloak.testsuite.docker.DockerClientTest] calling all TestCleanup
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5278361Z DockerClientTest ++ 11:16:40,576 INFO  [org.keycloak.testsuite.util.WaitUtils] Wait: 5000ms
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5279839Z DockerClientTest ++ 11:16:45,678 ERROR [org.keycloak.testsuite.docker.DockerClientTest] [DockerClientTest] shouldPerformDockerAuthAgainstRegistry() FAILED
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5281926Z DockerClientTest ++ 11:16:45,678 INFO  [org.keycloak.testsuite.docker.DockerClientTest] [DockerClientTest] shouldPerformDockerAuthAgainstRegistry() FINISHED

2025-11-11T11:18:53.5283503Z DockerClientTest ++ 
2025-11-11T11:18:53.5283895Z DockerClientTest ++ Log has ended
```

So I suppose docker is checking now the SANs extensions in the certificate and TLS is failing. This test creates a docker registry and configures it to use keycloak. So the IP is the host network in the runner. My vote is to use plain `http` for this test. 

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Test should work OK.

### Actual behavior

Test failure .

### How to Reproduce?

See for example this run: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/19263277490/job/55073417805?pr=44030

issue_title: Double-encoding of query parameter values (e.g. acr_values) for version 26.4
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

**Problem**

uriBuilder.queryParam() already performs URL encoding.

Manually applying URLEncoder.encode(parameter, StandardCharsets.UTF_8) before calling it results in double encoding, where:

space → "+" → "%2B"


Affected method:

uriBuilder.queryParam(forwardParameter, URLEncoder.encode(parameter, StandardCharsets.UTF_8));


Reference (line of code):
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/39964befefc41eec3330fcf5c94f5e1ea498d075/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/oidc/AbstractOAuth2IdentityProvider.java#L562

Related commit that introduced the issue:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/41755

Take note : It was working in version 26.0

### Version

26.4

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The URI component should only be encoded once.

### Actual behavior

The URI component are encoded twice.

### How to Reproduce?

Create an Identity Provider and add a forwarded query parameter, use a query parameter such as acr_values. You will notice that something like hello world turns into hello%2Bworld when it should be either +or %20.
 

### Anything else?

Reference (line of code):
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/39964befefc41eec3330fcf5c94f5e1ea498d075/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/oidc/AbstractOAuth2IdentityProvider.java#L562

Related commit that introduced the issue:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/41755
commit_message:
Fixing encoding of forwarded parameters

Closes #44125

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: [BUG] 'master' realm lockout due to NullPointerException when "Conditional 2FA" is moved before "Username Password Form"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

When editing the `master` realm's Browser authentication flow in Keycloak 26.4.4, if the "Conditional 2FA" execution (which includes WebAuthn/Passkey checks) is moved to a position *before* the "Username Password Form" execution, it becomes impossible to log in to the `master` realm. The login page shows a generic error, and the backend logs a fatal `NullPointerException`.

### Version

26.4.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The system should not crash with an NPE, even if the flow is misconfigured. Ideally, it should fail gracefully or skip the conditional check if the user context is not yet available. A misconfiguration should not lead to a complete and irrecoverable lockout of the `master` realm.

### Actual behavior

A `java.lang.NullPointerException` is thrown because the `user` object is `null`. The `WebAuthnAuthenticator` (triggered by "Conditional 2FA") attempts to access `user.credentialManager()`, but the user has not been identified by the "Username Password Form" step yet.

This critical error completely locks the administrator out of the `master` realm admin console, making it impossible to revert the faulty flow through the UI.

### How to Reproduce?

1.  Run Keycloak 26.4.4.
2.  Log in to the `master` realm admin console.
3.  Navigate to **Authentication** > **Flows**.
4.  Select the **`browser`** flow. (Or a copy of it, if bound).
5.  Move the execution for **"Conditional 2FA"** (or its parent conditional flow) to a position *above* the **"Username Password Form"** execution.
6.  Save the flow.
7.  Log out.
8.  Attempt to log back in to the `master` realm admin console.
9.  The login will fail, and the `NullPointerException` will appear in the logs.

### Anything else?

My original intent was to enable passwordless (passkey-only) login for my admin account, which already has a passkey registered. I assumed the passkey check would identify the user.

This bug is critical because it leads to a **lockout situation** in the `master` realm, with no obvious recovery method other than direct database manipulation.

issue_title: Disabling/enabling workflows result in validation errors and the name is missing from representation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Using the following workflow definition to create a workflow:

```
{
  "name": "disable inactive users",
  "on": "user-logged-in(account-console)",
  "concurrency": {
    "cancel-if-running": true
  },
  "if": "!has-role(realm-management/realm-admin)",
  "steps": [
    {
      "uses": "notify-user",
      "after": "30",
      "with": {
        "custom_message": "Your account can be disabled due to inactivity!"
      }
    },
    {
      "uses": "disable-user",
      "after": "30"
    }
  ]
}
```

and subsequently trying to switch it to disable results in validation error saying that only "enabled" and "name" can be updated. Aside from that, the name of workflow is now missing when we click on it to show the definition

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Enabling/disabling the workflow should work with no errors. The name should be preserved when fetching the workflow.

### Actual behavior

Changing the `enabled` status fails due to validation errors. Name is missing after that when fetching the specific workflow.

### How to Reproduce?

Create the workflow in the description. Then try to disable the workflow using the action item -> validation error.
After that click on the workflow to see the definition -> name is now missing.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: OIDC identity provider should allow to setup JWKS URL (or hardcoded keys) when JWT authorization grant enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

The option `Use JWKS URL` of OIDC Identity provider (and eventually also `JWKS URL`, `Validating public key` and `Validating public key ID` based on the value of `UseJWKS URL`) are shown only when `Validate Signatures` is ON. However when OIDC provider is used for the JWT authorization grant, the option `Use JWKS URL` (and other 3 options based on the value of that option) should be shown as well. As JWT authorization grant cannot work without the ability to set the identity provider keys (either through JWKS URL or hardcoded).

### Version

nightly (from 2025-11-14)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Administrator should be always able to setup JWKS URL (or hardcoded public key) when JWT authorization grant is enabled for OIDC IDP

### Actual behavior

Administrator is not able to setup JWKS URL (or hardcoded public key) when JWT authorization grant is enabled for OIDC IDP unless he also enables `Validate signatures` .

### How to Reproduce?

- Start Keycloak with enabled `jwt-authorization-grant` experimental feature
- Login to admin console and create OIDC identity provider
- Enable JWT authorization grant
- Try to setup JWKS URL or public-key for validate JWT authorization

### Anything else?

_No response_

Always allow to setup JWKS URL in oidc idp

Closes #44217

I am using the following command to run the latest nightly:

```bash
podman run --rm -ti \
	-v ./cache-ispn.xml:/opt/keycloak/conf/cache-ispn.xml:Z \
	-e 'KC_FEATURES_DISABLED=persistent-user-sessions' \
	-e 'KC_CACHE_CONFIG_FILE=cache-ispn.xml' \
keycloak/keycloak:nightly@sha256:7668e5b6bc3e90ea134980ef698b77e62033d4b0e535f10d8dc3562dbfafe5c2 \
	start-dev
```

A simplified version of cache-ispn.xml look like so:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<infinispan
        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:schemaLocation="urn:infinispan:config:15.0 http://www.infinispan.org/schemas/infinispan-config-15.0.xsd"
        xmlns="urn:infinispan:config:15.0">

    <cache-container name="keycloak">
        <transport lock-timeout="60000" stack="udp"/>
        <distributed-cache name="sessions" owners="2">
            <expiration lifespan="-1"/>
        </distributed-cache>
        <distributed-cache name="offlineSessions" owners="2">
            <expiration lifespan="-1"/>
        </distributed-cache>
        </cache-container>
</infinispan>
```

### Anything else?

The logging statement that throws the error from my logs is here:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f96765c4b49fd6a6ae392054def7ccd61e59d6fa/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/spi/infinispan/impl/embedded/CacheConfigurator.java#L290

I'm aware that my configuration is most likely very invalid and needs fixing, this issue is to report that Keycloak should log this properly instead of dumping a stack trace when it tries to write a log entry.

The error seems to have been introduced with 168d9cc09053c1c974023a088752db6dca12438d.

issue_title: [OID4VCI] JWT VC Issuer Metadata well-known endpoint should be available at /.well-known/jwt-vc-issuer/realms/{realm}
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

Similarly to the openid-credential-issuer metadata endpoint (see #41589), the jwt-vc-issuer metadata endpoint's URI should be constructed by inserting `/.well-known/jwt-vc-issuer` between the host/port and path component of the issuer's URI (as provided in the `iss` claim).
For instance, the jwt-vc-issuer metadata of `https://keycloak.example.com/realms/oid4vci` should be available at `https://keycloak.example.com/.well-known/jwt-vc-issuer/realms/oid4vci`.

The current version of Keycloak does not conform to this part of the draft and instead makes the metadata available at `https://keycloak.example.com/realms/oid4vci/.well-known/jwt-vc-issuer`. Attempting to access the metadata at the spec-defined location results in a 404 error.

Relevant part of the spec/draft: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-sd-jwt-vc-13#jwt-vc-issuer-metadata

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

See above

### Actual behavior

See above

### How to Reproduce?

- Create a realm with the verifiable credentials feature enabled
- Attempt to access the JWT VC Issuer Metadata Endpoint at the spec-defined location 

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add spec-compliant jwt vc issuer well-known endpoint

- expose /.well-known/jwt-vc-issuer/realms/{realm} and keep legacy route with deprecation headers
- build consumer metadata URL per draft-ietf-oauth-sd-jwt-vc-13 and add realm-path coverage
- add integration test for new path plus deprecation headers on legacy endpoint

Closes #44256

Signed-off-by: Awambeng Rodrick <awambengrodrick@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Awambeng <awambengrodrick@gmail.com>

issue_title: Client JWKS provided via Dynamic Client Registration is stored and used but not displayed in Admin Console Keys tab
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

When creating a client through Dynamic Client Registration (DCR) and providing an inline jwks object, Keycloak correctly stores the JWKS and uses it to verify private_key_jwt assertions.
However, the Admin Console does not display the JWKS on the Keys tab, even though:

The exported client JSON contains the JWKS

The Admin REST API returns the JWKS

JWT assertions signed with the private key are successfully verified by Keycloak

This appears to be a UI inconsistency or missing UI mapping for JWKS provided through DCR.

### Version

26.3.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The Keys tab should display the JWK from the client’s stored jwks field, or at least indicate that the client contains a JWKS provided via DCR.

### Actual behavior

- The Keys tab shows no keys at all.
- No JWKS is displayed in any of the UI sections.
- However, the JWKS is stored internally (GET /admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{id} includes it).
- JWT assertions using the corresponding private key are successfully validated, proving the backend is reading the   JWKS correctly.

This indicates the issue is limited to the Admin Console UI, not the backend verification logic.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start Keycloak (tested using official container image).
2. Create an Initial Access Token for the realm.
3. Use DCR to create a client with an inline JWKS. Example request:

```
  {
    "client_name": "test-nojwks",
    "grant_types": ["authorization_code", "client_credentials", "refresh_token"],
    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "tls_client_auth",
    "jwks": {
      "keys": [
        {
          "kty": "RSA",
          "kid": "kid",
          "alg": "PS256",
          "use": "sig",
          "n": "<omitted>",
          "e": "AQAB",
          "x5c": ["<certificate omitted>"]
        }
      ]
    },
    "redirect_uris": ["https://example.com/v1/callback"]
  }
```

4. Keycloak successfully creates the client.
5. Export the client or fetch via Admin REST API → JWKS is present.
6. Open the client in the Admin Console → Clients → sm-test-nojwks → Keys.

### Anything else?

The UI appears to only show keys created through the UI itself.
commit_message:
Show jwks, certificate or publicKey for the key information
Closes #44284

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: View-Group Permission shows Users in User-List
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/fine-grained-permissions ### Describe the bug A user with only the group view permission can see all members of that specific group in the Users list. The user does not have the view-members permission for that group. User details cannot be accessed, but the users from that group are still visible in the list. Users from groups where view-members is granted appear as expected. ### Version 26.4.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior The Users list should only display users who belong to groups for which the current admin user has the “view-members” permission (see the checked boxes in the screenshot). If the user has only the group “view” permission and no “view-members” permission for any group, the Users list should be empty. ### Actual behavior A user who only has the group view permission can still see all members of that group in the Users list of the admin-console, even though they do not have the view-members permission. Opening any of those users is blocked with an insufficient-permissions error; the user details page does not load. ### How to Reproduce? I have a group-Structure that look similar to the following structure: ``` /Company/Sub1/DepartmentA /Company/Sub1/DepartmentB /Company/Sub2/DepartmentC /Company/Sub2/DepartmentD ``` My goal is to allow certain Users of Sub1 and Sub2 to manage Users within their own Group and child-groups. "fine grained permissions" are enabled to achieve this goal. A "Sub"-user is granted manage-user permission for his group and child-groups. E.g. "Sub1", "DepartmentA" and "DepartmentB". In order to be able to see his groups the "Sub"-user was also granted a view-group permission for the Group "Company" In ended up with 3 permissions: - manage-user-Sub1 - Resource Type: Group - Scope: view, view-members, manage-members, manage-membership - Resources: "Sub1", "DepartmentA" and "DepartmentB". - Policy: allow-Sub1 - manage-user-Sub2 - Resource Type: Group - Scope: view, view-members, manage-members, manage-membership - Resources: "Sub1", "DepartmentA" and "DepartmentB". - Policy: allow-Sub2 - view-group - Resource Type: Group - Scope: view - Resources: "Company" - Policy: allow-Company I am using group based policies to grant this permissions to users. I have 3 Policies: - allow-Sub1 - Resource Type: Group - Resources: Sub1 - Logic : Positive - allow-Sub2 - Resource Type: Group - Resources: Sub2 - Logic : Positive - allow-Company - Resource Type: Group - Resources: Sub1, Sub2 - Logic : Positive I granted the "query-users" and the "query-groups" roles to the Sub-User to allow access to the User- and Group-Menu. A Sub-User is either added to the Group "Sub1" or the Group "Sub2". ### Anything else? Admin theme: keycloak.v2
commit_message:

issue_title: Logical error in Danish email verification message - tells users to ignore if they DID create account
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

translations

### Describe the bug

  The Danish email verification message contains a logical error that instructs users to do the opposite of what is
  intended. The message tells users to ignore the email if they DID create the account, when it should tell them to
  ignore it if they DIDN'T create the account.

  **Affected file:** `themes/src/main/resources-community/theme/base/email/messages/messages_da.properties`
  - Line 2: `emailVerificationBody` (plain text version)
  - Line 3: `emailVerificationBodyHtml` (HTML version)

### Version

26.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The Danish email verification message should say:
  > "Hvis det **ikke** var dig der har oprettet denne konto, bedes du se bort fra denne mail."

Translation: "If you did **not** create this account, just ignore this message"

### Actual behavior

The Danish email verification message currently says:
  > "Hvis det **var** dig der har oprettet denne konto, bedes du se bort fra denne mail."

Translation: "If you did create this account, just ignore this message"

### How to Reproduce?

 1. Configure Keycloak with Danish locale (`da`)
  2. Create a new user account that requires email verification
  3. Trigger an email verification email
  4. Check the received email in Danish
  5. Observe the incorrect logic in the final sentence

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix logical error in Danish email verification message

Added missing 'ikke' (not) to correctly instruct users to ignore
  the email if they did NOT create the account.

Fixes #44342

issue_title: 403 Forbidden when assigning realm-management client roles despite FGAP disabled (regression in 26.4.0+)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

In Keycloak 26.4.0 to 26.4.5, the Fine-Grained Admin Permissions (FGAP) code path executes even when FGAP is disabled at both realm and client levels. This causes service accounts with the create-realm role to receive 403 Forbidden errors when attempting to assign realm client roles to users via the Admin REST API.

This is a regression from 26.3.3, which worked correctly.

Environment
Keycloak Version: 26.4.0 to 26.4.5
Deployment: Kubernetes
Working Version: 26.3.3
Version
26.4.5

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Expected behavior
A service account with the realm roles should be able to assign realm-management client roles (e.g., view-users, manage-users, view-clients) to users when FGAP is disabled.


### Actual behavior

The operation fails with 403 Forbidden, despite FGAP being disabled.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create client for master realm and assign `create-realm` , `default-roles-master` to client and with FGAP disabled (adminPermissionsEnabled: false) 
2. Obtain token using the newly created client using token grant-type client credentials
3. Attempt to assign realm-management client roles to user via REST API:

```
POST /admin/realms/{realm}/users/{user-id}/role-mappings/clients/{client-id}
Authorization: Bearer {token}
Content-Type: application/json
  [
  {"id":"{role-id}","name":"view-users","composite":true,"clientRole":true,"containerId":"{containerId}"}
  ]
```

Observe 403 Forbidden error

### Anything else?

### Configuration Details

Realm FGAP Status:

```
  {
    "realm": "my-realm",
    "adminPermissionsEnabled": false,
    "userManagedAccessAllowed": false
  }
```

Realm-Management Client FGAP Status:

```
  {
    "error": "Feature not enabled",
    "error_description": "For more on this error consult the server log."
  }
```

Service Account Roles:

```
  {
  "realmMappings": [
    {
      "id": "f2e97d52-c077-4514-8342-1dfbb3731444",
      "name": "default-roles-master",
      "description": "${role_default-roles}",
      "composite": true,
      "clientRole": false,
      "containerId": "ec9abd99-061d-454e-bee3-3f11db751f65"
    },
    {
      "id": "601cb2c2-3e51-4e74-b7f8-d06f1231f9d4",

```

#### Additional Context

This affects both direct client role assignments and composite roles containing client roles
The same operation works successfully via the Keycloak Admin Console UI when assigned admin role to master realm client.
This appears to be a regression introduced between 26.3.x and 26.4.x

#### Impact

This blocks automation tools from managing realm-management role assignments using service accounts, forcing manual intervention through the Admin Console.

#### Hint

This issue looks like similar [issue](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/43579) which was fixed with 26.4.4 but this one is for users instead of groups. Therefore, it must be considered separate issue than [issue](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/43579) and need fix.

issue_title: Unlocalized date format in (email messages)
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area translations ### Describe the bug When using date parameters in the `msg` formatter, they are not formatted according to the user locale. Freemarker wraps the Java `Date` object in [`SimpleData`](https://freemarker.apache.org/docs/api/freemarker/template/SimpleDate.html) object and the `MessageFormatterMethod` considers it a `SimpleScalar`, which is converted to a `String`. see: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/theme/beans/MessageFormatterMethod.java#L72-L73 While Freemarker would use the locale to format a date, this is no longer possible with the stringified date. A simple fix could look like: ``` if (item instanceof SimpleDate) { item = ((SimpleDate) item).getAsDate(); } else if (item instanceof SimpleScalar) { item = ((SimpleScalar) item).getAsString(); } ``` ### Version 26.4.2 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Emails contain a correctly formatted date according the users locale. In the example case, the format should be something like `21.11.25, 09:46`. ### Actual behavior Receiving a mail containing a date, it is not correctly formatted to the users locale. An example with a German locale setting: ### How to Reproduce? - Activate "email" event listener in realm settings -> Events - Configure realm email settings so you can receive emails - Configure (additionally) German as a supported realm language - Configure a user with an email address which you can access - Login as a realm user into account console - Set user locale to German - Update password ("Anmeldung" -> "Aktualisieren") - An email was sent to the user which contains the formatted date, but with a non-German format ### Anything else? I am planning to provide a pull request for this.
commit_message:
fix: handle localized date formatting in message format

Closes #44377

issue_title: Node.js admin client does not refresh tokens
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/client-js

### Describe the bug

The Node.js admin client obtain an initial access token, but does not check or refresh this token later. This will cause issues when using the admin client for a longer period of time.

As an example this causes the Account E2E tests to be flaky as they sometimes can take longer to execute than the validity of the token. Especially in private repositories.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Admin client automatically refreshes the access token if it is close to expiring. A default of 5 seconds minimum validity would be sensible.

### Actual behavior

The admin client never refreshes the access token. A custom token provider can be used to do this, but that is  not easy to implement.

### How to Reproduce?

Run Account E2E tests in a private repository, or simple call `auth` then wait for more than 5 minutes before calling any operations through the admin endpoints.

### Anything else?

_No response_

added ability to refresh token when within time (#45789)

issue_title: Delete user step in workflows is not removing other scheduled steps the user might have
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

If a user has multiple scheduled steps, and one of the workflows runs the `delete-user` step, the user is removed from the local DB but the other scheduled steps/workflows remain in the database.

This happens because the step is not running the deletion through `UserManager`, which is necessary to trigger the event that is used to ensure the workflow state table is cleaned up as well.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When a workflow removes a user through the `delete-user` step, all other scheduled steps associated with the user in the workflow state table should be removed as well.

### Actual behavior

When user is removed by the `delete-user` step, other scheduled steps in the workflow state table are not being removed.

### How to Reproduce?

Setup two workflows with scheduled tasks. Ensure one of them has the `delete-user` step, and that this step runs before the other workflow's scheduled step (can be any step). Once the user is removed, check the state table - the other scheduled step is still there.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Ensure delete step is triggering UserRemovedEvent

Closes #44398

issue_title: Workflows are restarting on any event instead of the one that activates it
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When we have a workflow with concurrency setting `cancel-if-running=true` are restarting on any event when they should be restarting only if the event matches the workflow's `on` condition.

Example:
```
name: disable inactive users
enabled: true
on: user-logged-in(account-console)
concurrency:
  cancel-if-running: true
if: "!has-role(admin)"
steps:
- uses: notify-user
  with:
    custom_message: Your account can be disabled due to inactivity
- uses: disable-user
  after: 300
- uses: delete-user
  after: 300
```

This workflow is triggered only when the user logs in to the account-console client. The concurrency setting is used to specify that the workflow should be cancelled/restarted when the same event is received again - i.e. user logs back in to the account-console. However, the current implementation is restarting the workflow on ANY event supported by Workflows.


### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Workflow should only be restarted if the `on` condition is satisfied by the event being processed. Events unrelated to the activation of the workflow should not have an impact on its execution.

### Actual behavior

When a workflow has a concurrency setting, any event is causing the workflow to be restarted.

### How to Reproduce?

Create the workflow in the description. Then log into the account console with any non-admin user. The workflow will run, executing the notify step immediately, and then scheduling the second task (disable user). Now make the same user join a group, which will fire a group membership added event. Notice that the workflow is restarted - the first step is run again, and the second step is re-scheduled.

The workflow should only restart when the users logs into the account again. Any other event should be discarded.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure workflow is only restarted on events that match the activation condition

Closes #44399

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: Removing a user's group is not activating workflows that use the user-group-membership-removed event
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

If we define a workflow that activates on the `user-group-membership-removed` event, the workflow is never activated. This happens because Keycloak's  `GroupMemberLeaveEvent` doesn't have the user who left the group, which causes the `WorkflowEventListener` to fail to build the corresponding workflow event as the resource id (in this case the user id) is required.

We need to ensure the `GroupMemberLeaveEvent` has a reference to the user whose role was revoked so that the corresponding workflow event can be built.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Workflow with `on: user-group-membership-removed(somegroup)` should activate when the user leaves the group.

### Actual behavior

Workflow with `on: user-group-membership-removed(somegroup)` is not activating when the user leaves the group.

### How to Reproduce?

Create any workflow with `on: user-group-membership-removed(somegroup)`. Add the user as a member of the referenced group. Then remove the user from the group - the workflow should activate but it doesn't.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure GroupMemberLeaveEvent has a reference to the user leaving the group

Closes #44400

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: DCR endpoint ignores client's requested token_endpoint_auth_method
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

## Summary

Keycloak's Dynamic Client Registration (DCR) endpoint ignores the client's requested `token_endpoint_auth_method` and always returns `client_secret_basic`, even when clients explicitly request `client_secret_post`.

## Impact

This breaks compatibility with OAuth clients that require `client_secret_post`, including:
- Model Context Protocol (MCP) clients (per RFC 9110 requirement)
- Clients that cannot support HTTP Basic authentication
- Environments with security policies requiring POST-based authentication

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Per [RFC 7591 Section 2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591#section-2), the `token_endpoint_auth_method` field indicates authentication method for the token endpoint requested by the client. Since `client_secret_post` is a standard method supported by Keycloak, the server should honor this request.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak always returns `client_secret_basic` regardless of the client's request, effectively ignoring the parameter.

### How to Reproduce?

### Setup Keycloak

Use the preconfigured test realm from FastMCP (includes DCR enabled and trusted hosts configured):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/stephaneberle9/fastmcp.git
cd fastmcp
git checkout feat/keycloak-auth-provider
cd examples/auth/keycloak_auth/keycloak
./start-keycloak.sh  # Imports realm with DCR enabled
```

Setup details: [README.md](https://github.com/stephaneberle9/fastmcp/blob/feat/keycloak-auth-provider/examples/auth/keycloak_auth/keycloak/README.md)

### Test with cURL

```bash
# DCR request explicitly requesting client_secret_post
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/realms/fastmcp/clients-registrations/openid-connect \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "client_name": "Test Client",
    "redirect_uris": ["http://localhost:8000/callback"],
    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_post"
  }'
```

**Expected:** Response includes `"token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_post"`
**Actual:** Response includes `"token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic"`

## Test Resources

Complete reproduction environment available in FastMCP repo (branch `feat/keycloak-auth-provider`):
- [realm-fastmcp.json](https://github.com/stephaneberle9/fastmcp/blob/feat/keycloak-auth-provider/examples/auth/keycloak_auth/keycloak/realm-fastmcp.json) - Test realm configuration
- [README.md](https://github.com/stephaneberle9/fastmcp/blob/feat/keycloak-auth-provider/examples/auth/keycloak_auth/keycloak/README.md) - Setup instructions

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
DCR endpoint ignores client's requested token_endpoint_auth_method in case it is client_secret_post
closes #44403

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: Security issue with Organization feature exposes and fills the account name automatically in user/password form
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

Hi,

This is a security issue using the Organization feature.

The issue is that if a user enters its email address, then the users account name will be prefilled in the username/password form, as the entered email address is translated into an account name.
So one can document all the accounts by just knowing the email-address.

Recommendation to fix this issue:
Dont visualize any data in the username/password form.

In our case the users account name (username) is sensitive; so please don't expose any other data then the user enters.

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Dont expose any other data then the user enters.

### Actual behavior

The users username is presented / exposed instead of what has been entered from the user.

### How to Reproduce?

Enable Organization feature. 
Enter an email address
The users account name is presented and prefilled in the username/password form

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: findClientSessionsClientIds performance issue
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

I have recently observed, that Read IOPS of my Keycloak clusters database occasionally spikes to very high levels.

Debugging showed, that the named query `findClientSessionsClientIds` is responsible for these spikes.

An `explain` of the query verified this - `lastSessionRefresh 0` is used as a dummy value:

    keycloak=> explain analyze select pcse1_0.CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.EXTERNAL_CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.CLIENT_STORAGE_PROVIDER,count(*) from public.OFFLINE_CLIENT_SESSION pcse1_0 join public.OFFLINE_USER_SESSION puse1_0 on pcse1_0.USER_SESSION_ID=puse1_0.USER_SESSION_ID and puse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG=pcse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG where puse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG='1' and puse1_0.REALM_ID='Customers' and puse1_0.LAST_SESSION_REFRESH>=0 group by pcse1_0.CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.EXTERNAL_CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.CLIENT_STORAGE_PROVIDER;
                                                                                           QUERY PLAN
    ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
     Finalize GroupAggregate  (cost=741482.34..741488.14 rows=22 width=57) (actual time=9226.410..9323.312 rows=6 loops=1)
       Group Key: pcse1_0.client_id, pcse1_0.external_client_id, pcse1_0.client_storage_provider
       ->  Gather Merge  (cost=741482.34..741487.48 rows=44 width=57) (actual time=9226.395..9323.296 rows=18 loops=1)
             Workers Planned: 2
             Workers Launched: 2
             ->  Sort  (cost=740482.32..740482.37 rows=22 width=57) (actual time=9196.666..9196.951 rows=6 loops=3)
                   Sort Key: pcse1_0.client_id, pcse1_0.external_client_id, pcse1_0.client_storage_provider
                   Sort Method: quicksort  Memory: 25kB
                   Worker 0:  Sort Method: quicksort  Memory: 25kB
                   Worker 1:  Sort Method: quicksort  Memory: 25kB
                   ->  Partial HashAggregate  (cost=740481.61..740481.83 rows=22 width=57) (actual time=9196.630..9196.916 rows=6 loops=3)
                         Group Key: pcse1_0.client_id, pcse1_0.external_client_id, pcse1_0.client_storage_provider
                         Batches: 1  Memory Usage: 24kB
                         Worker 0:  Batches: 1  Memory Usage: 24kB
                         Worker 1:  Batches: 1  Memory Usage: 24kB
                         ->  Parallel Hash Join  (cost=336131.55..727419.06 rows=1306255 width=49) (actual time=4632.833..8678.519 rows=1151082 loops=3)
                               Hash Cond: ((pcse1_0.user_session_id)::text = (puse1_0.user_session_id)::text)
                               ->  Parallel Seq Scan on offline_client_session pcse1_0  (cost=0.00..337206.07 rows=1435597 width=88) (actual time=0.028..1324.107 rows=1154159 loops=3)
                                     Filter: ((offline_flag)::text = '1'::text)
                                     Rows Removed by Filter: 26811
                               ->  Parallel Hash  (cost=307384.82..307384.82 rows=1415178 width=39) (actual time=1824.144..1824.145 rows=1151082 loops=3)
                                     Buckets: 131072  Batches: 64  Memory Usage: 4896kB
                                     ->  Parallel Seq Scan on offline_user_session puse1_0  (cost=0.00..307384.82 rows=1415178 width=39) (actual time=0.010..1250.316 rows=1151082 loops=3)
                                           Filter: ((last_session_refresh >= 0) AND ((offline_flag)::text = '1'::text) AND ((realm_id)::text = 'Customers'::text))
                                           Rows Removed by Filter: 94081
     Planning Time: 40.232 ms
     Execution Time: 9323.381 ms
    (27 rows)


`offline_client_session` has a partially fitting index: `"constraint_offl_cl_ses_pk3" PRIMARY KEY, btree (user_session_id, client_id, client_storage_provider, external_client_id, offline_flag)`

`offline_user_session` has a perfect fitting index: `"idx_offline_uss_by_last_session_refresh" btree (realm_id, offline_flag, last_session_refresh)`

But Postgres decides against their use. I've tried adding a better fitting index on `offline_client_session`, but it actually made it worse.

Do you see optimization potential or should I scale up the database? The tables are 2500 MB and 2200 MB, so quite large already. 

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

result in < 1 second

### Actual behavior

> 9 seconds

### How to Reproduce?

call `/{realm}/client-session-stats` of a realm with many sessions

Side question: Did this get removed from the UI?

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: NPE when importing SAML EntityDescriptor without SPSSODescriptor
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

When importing a SAML EntityDescriptor via the SAML Entity Descriptor Client Registration endpoint, Keycloak throws a NullPointerException if the uploaded metadata does not contain an SPSSODescriptor.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Error handling

### Actual behavior

Keycloak returns server error:
```
Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.dom.saml.v2.metadata.SPSSODescriptorType.isWantAssertionsSigned()" because "spDescriptorType" is null
        at org.keycloak.protocol.saml.EntityDescriptorDescriptionConverter.loadEntityDescriptors(EntityDescriptorDescriptionConverter.java:193)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.saml.EntityDescriptorDescriptionConverter.convertToInternal(EntityDescriptorDescriptionConverter.java:73)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.saml.clientregistration.EntityDescriptorClientRegistrationProvider.createSaml(EntityDescriptorClientRegistrationProvider.java:47)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.saml.clientregistration.EntityDescriptorClientRegistrationProvider$quarkusrestinvoker$createSaml_7b43b364c4f60a385ea6d147f84361448f568d5a.invoke(Unknown Source)

        at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:183)

        at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2630)
        at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor.runThreadBody(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1622)

issue_title: Intermittent ConcurrentModificationException during SAML initialization causing status code 400 for clients
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

When several requests trigger SAML-related initialization concurrently, a ConcurrentModificationException is occasionally thrown from the SAML module. The stack traces lead back to calls made during the creation of ThreadLocal `DocumentBuilder` instances.

### Version

latest

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should start consistently without intermittent exceptions, even when many threads initialize their ThreadLocal `DocumentBuilder` instances at the same time.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak startup sometimes fails with a `ConcurrentModificationException` originating from the XML parsing components used by the SAML module. When this occurs, the client attempting to authenticate receives an `HTTP 400` response. The issue is intermittent and typically appears only during multi-threaded startup conditions.

### How to Reproduce?

A JUnit test that spawns multiple threads and repeatedly triggers ThreadLocal initialization of the XML `DocumentBuilder` can expose the issue. The test does not fail on every run—the bug is timing-dependent—but repeatedly running the test will at some point expose the bug where the `ConcurrentModificationException` is thrown, demonstrating that the problem exists.

### Anything else?

I will provide a PR with the failing test and a proposed fix.
commit_message:
Fix race condition in SAML DocumentBuilderFactory creation

Closes #44438

issue_title: ClassCastException on mixing AddressMapper with ClaimMapper
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

If the Keycloak internal AddressMapper is used in conjunction with a ClaimMapper that tries to add an additional attribute to the address-structure we get a ClassCastException here:

```java
public class OIDCAttributeMapperHelper {

private static void mapClaim(List<String> split, Object attributeValue, Map<String, Object> jsonObject, boolean isMultivalued) {
          ....
          else {
                @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
                Map<String, Object> nested = (Map<String, Object>) jsonObject.get(component);

                if (nested == null) {
                    nested = new HashMap<>();
                    jsonObject.put(component, nested);
                }

                jsonObject = nested;
            }
        }
}
...
}
```

The reason is that the AddessMapper creates an object of `AddressClaimSet` that is added into the token, while the ClaimAttributeMapper is working with a `Map<String, Object>`.

```
2025-11-25 07:54:17,716 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-33) Uncaught server error: java.lang.ClassCastException: class org.keycloak.representations.AddressClaimSet cannot be cast to class java.util.Map (org.keycloak.representations.AddressClaimSet is in unnamed module of loader io.quarkus.bootstrap.runner.RunnerClassLoader @4bec1f0c; java.util.Map is in module java.base of loader 'bootstrap')
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.mapClaim(OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.java:345)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.mapClaim(OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.java:317)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.mapClaim(OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.java:278)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.UserAttributeMapper.setClaim(UserAttributeMapper.java:103)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper.setClaim(AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper.java:159)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper.transformUserInfoToken(AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper.java:80)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.TokenManager$3.applyMapper(TokenManager.java:821)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.TokenManager$3.applyMapper(TokenManager.java:818)
```



### Version

26.4.5 and before

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Both ClaimMappers should work together and create an extended address-object.
In our specific case we want to add a `type`-claim into the `address`-claim:

```json
"address": {
  ...,
  "type": "STRUCTURED"
}
```

### Actual behavior

An exception occurs and we get a 500 internal server error

### How to Reproduce?

see description

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When two IDP group mappers assign the same group and one decides to map the group and the other doesnt, there is currently a condition where sometimes the user is in the group and sometimes it is not. This is due to the following change: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/bb9015a1f26ada048610e8b89f34c0f3959ac9a9 / #43682
Where `joinGroup()` is missing the condition `&& updated == null` causing it to miss other updates to the user done in the same request.

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If two group mappers map the same group and one wants to join and one wants to leave, the join always wins.

### Actual behavior

Race condition, sometimes user joins, sometimes he doesn't, depending on mapper execution order.

### How to Reproduce?

Skipping as discussed with @ahus1 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: "Update email" AIA: "Back to Application" URL invokes OIDC callback with missing parameters
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

With the newly-supported `UPDATE_EMAIL` Application-Initiated Action (documented [here](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/26.4.6/server_admin/#_update-email-workflow)), we can trigger the "update email" flow from within our application.

This is very similar to `UPDATE_PASSWORD`. In fact, from the app's POV, there isn't any difference between invoking `UPDATE_EMAIL` and `UPDATE_PASSWORD`: You redirect to the OIDC authorization endpoint with `kc_action` set, Keycloak takes over for a while and guides the user through some process, and in the end, Keycloak redirects back to the app's OIDC callback URL.

However, with `UPDATE_EMAIL`, when the user is finally redirected back to the app after having verified their new email address, all OIDC parameters are missing. The callback endpoint is invoked without any parameters, like `https://my.app/oidc-callback`.

### Version

26.4.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When clicking the link in the "Verify new email" email, and then "Back to Application" on the Keycloak success screen, Keycloak should redirect to the OIDC callback with correct parameters (just like after a normal login).

If the user clicks on the "Verify new email" link when they are not logged in in Keycloak (e.g. they open it in a different browser, or at a later time), then the verification _still succeeds_ (this is correct).
Clicking "Back to Application" should then ideally first go to the Keycloak login screen (without going through the app at all), and only then redirect back to the app with correct OIDC params.

### Actual behavior

After a user confirms their new email address, they are redirected to the app's OIDC callback without any parameters.

The app then likely shows an error to the user, since this scenario is not valid (should never happen) according to the OIDC spec.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable the `UPDATE_EMAIL` feature for a realm with email verification turned on.
2. Create a simple web app using OIDC / Keycloak (all standard).
3. Log in to the app via Keycloak.
4. (optional) Verify that Application-Initiated Actions work in general by adding and invoking an `UPDATE_PASSWORD` AIA. Observe that your OIDC callback endpoint is correctly invoked.
5. Add and invoke an `UPDATE_EMAIL` AIA.
6. Enter a new email address and confirm.
7. Click the verification link in the email you just got.
8. You should see an "Email updated" informational screen by Keycloak. It also contains a link, "Back to Application". Click it.
9. Observe that you are redirected to your OIDC callback _without any parameters_.
commit_message:
Update email AIA: Back to Application URL invokes OIDC callback with missing parameters

Closes #44488

Signed-off-by: Martin Kanis <mkanis@redhat.com>
